workstream.name: ts2pant
workstream.unlocks: Functions that call helpers become translatable. Combined with milestone 1, covers the majority of 'compute and return' patterns. Pure-call const inlining enables const x = Math.max(a, b) and similar patterns.

problemStatement: ts2pant currently rejects any function body containing a non-special function call. Only .includes(), .filter(), and .map() are handled; all other calls produce UNSUPPORTED markers. Additionally, expressionHasSideEffects treats ALL calls as side-effectful, preventing const-binding inlining for patterns like const max = Math.max(a, b). Together, these are the #2 blocker in the workstream analysis, rejecting any function that delegates to helpers, uses Math.*, or binds call results to local constants.
solutionSummary: Two complementary changes. First, extend translateCallExpr with a general fallback encoding arbitrary calls as uninterpreted function applications (EUF theory, Kroening & Strichman Ch. 4): foo(a, b) → 'foo a b', obj.method(a) → 'method obj a'. Second, introduce a conservative purity oracle (syntactic effect analysis, Cousot & Cousot POPL 1977 — trivial instance over a {pure, effectful} lattice) with three tiers: (1a) known-pure builtin allowlist (Math.*, non-mutating String/Array methods), (1b) Effect-TS awareness (constructing Effect<A,E,R> is always pure — referential transparency by design), (1c) conservative default (unknown = effectful). The oracle is wired into expressionHasSideEffects to enable const-binding inlining for pure calls.

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flags needed. This is a CLI translator tool, not a web service.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Two independent tracks merge at Patch 4. Track A (Patches 1-2): general call translation. Track B (Patch 3): purity oracle infrastructure. Patch 4 wires the oracle into const inlining (needs both tracks). Patch 5 cleans up fixtures. Patches 1 and 3 can execute in parallel.

currentStateAnalysis: translateCallExpr in translate-body.ts (line 878) handles only .includes() → membership and .filter()/.map() → comprehensions. The catch-all at line 938 returns { unsupported: expr.getText() }. The ast.app() constructor exists and is used for property access (a.balance → balance a), so wasm infrastructure is ready. Separately, expressionHasSideEffects (line 512) returns true for ALL ts.isCallExpression nodes — this gates const-binding inlining via the shared inlineConstBindings function. The function takes no TypeChecker parameter, so type-based purity analysis requires threading checker through. Effect-TS is used in some target codebases; its Effect<A,E,R> type provides a structural signal for purity (construction is always referentially transparent).

requiredChanges.description: Replace the catch-all unsupported return in translateCallExpr with general call handlers: (1) method calls after special cases → ast.app(var(methodName), [receiver, ...args]), (2) free function calls with identifier callee → ast.app(var(fnName), args) or ast.var(fnName) for 0-arity. Reject spread arguments.

New module: conservative purity oracle with three tiers. Tier 1a: known-pure builtin allowlist (PURE_NAMESPACES, PURE_METHODS_BY_TYPE, PURE_ARRAY_METHODS, HO_PURE_ARRAY_METHODS). Tier 1b: Effect-TS awareness (detect Effect<A,E,R> return type via EffectTypeId branded property; known-pure Effect combinators; known-impure runners). Tier 1c: conservative default.
requiredChanges.signature: function isKnownPureCall(expr: ts.CallExpression, checker: ts.TypeChecker): boolean

requiredChanges.description: Add required checker parameter to expressionHasSideEffects. For CallExpression nodes, delegate to isKnownPureCall. Thread checker through all recursive calls and update every call site to pass checker.
requiredChanges.signature: function expressionHasSideEffects(expr: ts.Expression, checker: ts.TypeChecker): boolean

requiredChanges.description: New fixture file: free call, multi-arg, zero-arity, nested, call-in-arithmetic, call-with-prop-arg, method call, call-in-comparison. Plus call patterns moved from unsupported.ts (callInReturn, bubbleNegation, bubbleCondition).
New fixture file for pure-call const inlining: const-with-math, const-with-string-method, const-with-chained-pure-calls, const-with-impure-call (still rejected).
Remove callInReturn, bubbleNegation, bubbleCondition (now translatable). Keep destructuredParam, multiParamCallback, conditionalAssign, loopAssign, arrowWithLocals.

acceptanceCriteria: translateCallExpr handles general free function calls foo(a, b) as ast.app(ast.var('foo'), [a', b'])
translateCallExpr handles general method calls obj.method(a) as ast.app(ast.var('method'), [obj', a'])
Spread arguments produce UNSUPPORTED with clear message
isKnownPureCall returns true for Math.* calls
isKnownPureCall returns true for non-mutating String/Array prototype methods
isKnownPureCall returns true for calls returning Effect<A,E,R> (detected via EffectTypeId branded property)
isKnownPureCall returns true for known Effect combinators (pipe, flow, Effect.map, etc.)
isKnownPureCall returns false for Effect.runSync/runPromise/runFork (runners are impure)
isKnownPureCall returns false for unknown calls (conservative default)
expressionHasSideEffects accepts optional checker parameter; when provided, uses purity oracle for call expressions
const x = Math.max(a, b); return x + 1 now inlines and translates
const x = unknownFn(); return x is still rejected (conservative)
All existing tests pass, new fixture and unit tests pass

explicitOpinions.opinion: Encode all calls as uninterpreted functions (EUF theory)
explicitOpinions.rationale: Standard approach from Kroening & Strichman Ch. 4. The only axiom is congruence (equal args → equal results). Sound, simple, composable. If a matching Pantagruel rule exists, the solver uses its constraints; otherwise universally quantified.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Method receiver is the first argument (curried encoding)
explicitOpinions.rationale: Already the pattern for property access: a.balance → balance a. Consistent encoding.
foo() is a 0-arity function application — in Pantagruel, just a variable reference (EUF constant).

explicitOpinions.opinion: Three-tier conservative purity oracle, not whole-program effect inference
explicitOpinions.rationale: Full effect inference (Lucassen & Gifford, POPL 1988) requires whole-program analysis and effect polymorphism. A curated allowlist (Tier 1a) plus Effect-TS structural detection (Tier 1b) plus conservative default (Tier 1c) gives us practical coverage with zero false positives. This is a trivial instance of abstract interpretation (Cousot & Cousot, POPL 1977) over a {pure, effectful} lattice.

Effect<A,E,R> values are inert descriptions, not computations. This is referential transparency by design — the same guarantee as Haskell's IO monad. Even Effect.sync(() => sideEffect()) is pure at the construction level; the thunk is not invoked until a run* function executes it. Effect.runSync/runPromise/runFork are explicitly EXCLUDED from the pure set.
Pantagruel has no varargs. Spread cannot be resolved to fixed arity statically.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Purity oracle lives in a dedicated module (src/purity.ts)
explicitOpinions.rationale: The allowlist data is large, the concern is self-contained, and it can grow independently (Tier 2 body inspection in a future milestone). Clean separation from translation logic.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Math.max(a, b) translates to 'max Math a b' (include receiver); namespace-stripping is a follow-up
explicitOpinions.rationale: Including the receiver is sound under EUF — the solver treats 'max Math a b' as an uninterpreted application. Namespace-stripping (detecting Math/JSON/Object as pure namespaces and dropping the receiver) is a readability optimization documented as a known follow-up in the workstream doc.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Optional calls foo?.() are rejected with message pointing to milestone 6
explicitOpinions.rationale: Optional chaining is scoped to milestone 6 (remaining operators and nullish). Rejecting here with a clear UNSUPPORTED message avoids partial implementation.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Effect-TS type detection uses EffectTypeId branded property (robust, not source-path heuristic)
explicitOpinions.rationale: The EffectTypeId is a unique symbol branded on every Effect<A,E,R> type. Detecting it via the TypeChecker's structural type resolution is version-resilient and unforgeable. Source-file path matching (checking for 'effect/' in node_modules) is fragile across monorepo layouts and package managers.

explicitOpinions.opinion: checker (ts.TypeChecker) is a required parameter on expressionHasSideEffects, not optional
explicitOpinions.rationale: The TypeChecker is a fundamental component needed for type-based purity analysis, guard detection, and future milestones. Making it required (not optional) ensures all call sites provide it and prevents silent degradation to the conservative path. All current callers either have checker in scope or are reachable from translateBody which has it.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Arrow function bodies are checked for purity in higher-order array method callbacks
explicitOpinions.rationale: arr.filter(x => foo(x)) is pure only if foo is in the allowlist. The purity oracle must recurse into arrow function bodies (both expression-bodied and block-bodied with single return) to check for side effects. Without this, higher-order array methods would always be classified as impure, defeating the purpose of including them in the allowlist.

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_GENERAL_CALLS_PATCH_1.

> Test fixtures and stubs only. No behavioral invariants.

CallExpression.

---

patches.title: Implement general function call translation via EUF encoding

files.description: Extend translateCallExpr with general call handlers after special cases
Unskip and implement unit tests for general call translation
Snapshot expectations update — general calls now produce translated output

changes: In translateCallExpr, INSIDE the ts.isPropertyAccessExpression block (after .includes/.filter/.map), add general method call handler: reject spread args, translate receiver and args via translateBodyExpr, return ast.app(ast.var(methodName), [receiver, ...translatedArgs])

In translateCallExpr, AFTER the property access block, add general free function call handler for ts.isIdentifier callees: reject spread args, if 0 args return ast.var(fnName) (using paramNames mapping), else translate args and return ast.app(ast.var(fnName), translatedArgs)
Keep final return { unsupported: expr.getText() } for non-identifier, non-property-access callees (computed calls, tagged templates, etc.)
Unskip and implement 4 unit tests: free call → ast.app, method call → receiver as first arg, zero-arity → ast.var, spread → unsupported
Run npm run test:update-snapshots — expressions-calls.ts expectations change from UNSUPPORTED to Pantagruel; unsupported.ts expectations for callInReturn/bubbleNegation/bubbleCondition also change
testStubsImplemented: translateCallExpr > should translate free function call as uninterpreted application
translateCallExpr > should translate method call with receiver as first argument
translateCallExpr > should translate zero-arity call as variable reference
translateCallExpr > should reject spread arguments with UNSUPPORTED
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_GENERAL_CALLS_PATCH_2.

> General function calls translate as uninterpreted
> function applications (EUF theory).
> Ref: Kroening & Strichman, Decision Procedures, Ch. 4.

CallExpression.

special-cased? c: CallExpression => Bool.
has-spread? c: CallExpression => Bool.
translated? c: CallExpression => Bool.

---

all c: CallExpression, special-cased? c |
  translated? c.

all c: CallExpression, ~special-cased? c, ~has-spread? c |
  translated? c.

patches.title: Create purity oracle module with allowlist and Effect-TS tier

files.description: Conservative purity oracle. Three tiers: (1a) known-pure builtins allowlist, (1b) Effect-TS type detection, (1c) conservative default. Exports isKnownPureCall.
Unit tests for purity oracle: allowlist entries, Effect-TS detection, conservative default, impure runners
Fixture file for pure-call const inlining patterns: Math.max, String.indexOf, chained pure, impure-still-rejected

changes: Create src/purity.ts with isKnownPureCall(expr: ts.CallExpression, checker: ts.TypeChecker): boolean
Tier 1a — PURE_NAMESPACES: Set(['Math']) — all methods on these namespaces are pure

Tier 1a — PURE_METHODS_BY_TYPE: Map with entries for 'string' (indexOf, slice, substring, includes, startsWith, endsWith, trim, toLowerCase, toUpperCase, charAt, charCodeAt, split, replace, replaceAll, repeat, padStart, padEnd, match, search, concat, normalize), 'number' (toFixed, toPrecision, toString, toExponential, toLocaleString)
Tier 1a — PURE_ARRAY_METHODS: Set(['at', 'concat', 'flat', 'includes', 'indexOf', 'join', 'keys', 'lastIndexOf', 'slice', 'toString', 'values']) — non-mutating, no callback

Tier 1a — HO_PURE_ARRAY_METHODS: Set(['every', 'filter', 'find', 'findIndex', 'flatMap', 'map', 'reduce', 'reduceRight', 'some']) — pure if callback arg is side-effect-free. For arrow function callbacks, recurse into the body: expression-bodied arrows check the expression; block-bodied arrows with a single return check the return expression. Other callback shapes (function expressions, identifiers) are conservatively treated as impure

Tier 1b — isEffectReturningCall: check if the callee's return type has the EffectTypeId branded property (structural detection via checker). Known-pure Effect combinators: pipe, flow, identity from effect/Function. Known-IMPURE runners: Effect.runSync, runPromise, runSyncExit, runPromiseExit, runFork — if callee matches a runner, return false even though it may return Effect
Tier 1c — default: return false (conservative, treat as effectful)
Use ts.TypeFlags.StringLike and ts.TypeFlags.NumberLike for primitive type detection; checker.isArrayType for arrays
For namespace detection: check ts.isIdentifier(receiver) && PURE_NAMESPACES.has(receiver.text)
Create tests/purity.test.mts with unit tests covering each tier

Create expressions-const-pure-calls.ts fixture with 4+ exported functions: constMathMax (const m = Math.max(a,b); return m+1), constStringMethod (const i = s.indexOf('x'); return i), constChainedPure (const a = Math.abs(x); const b = Math.max(a, y); return b), constImpureCall (const r = unknownFn(x); return r — should still UNSUPPORTED)
Run npm run test:update-snapshots (pure-call fixtures will initially show rejected-const-binding expectations since purity oracle is not yet wired in)

isKnownPureCall > should return false for array.filter with impure callback

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_GENERAL_CALLS_PATCH_3.

> Purity oracle: conservative syntactic effect analysis.
> Ref: Cousot & Cousot, Abstract Interpretation, POPL 1977.
> Trivial instance: lattice {pure, effectful}, default = effectful.

CallExpression.
PurityTier.

builtin-allowlist => PurityTier.
effect-ts-aware => PurityTier.
conservative-default => PurityTier.

tier c: CallExpression => PurityTier.
pure? c: CallExpression => Bool.

---

all c: CallExpression, tier c = builtin-allowlist |
  pure? c.

all c: CallExpression, tier c = effect-ts-aware |
  pure? c.

all c: CallExpression, tier c = conservative-default |
  ~pure? c.

patches.title: Wire purity oracle into expressionHasSideEffects for const inlining

files.description: Make checker a required parameter on expressionHasSideEffects. For CallExpression nodes, delegate to isKnownPureCall. Thread checker through all call sites — every caller must provide it.
Snapshot expectations for expressions-const-pure-calls.ts change from rejected to translated output

changes: Import isKnownPureCall from ./purity.js in translate-body.ts
Change expressionHasSideEffects signature to (expr: ts.Expression, checker: ts.TypeChecker): boolean — checker is REQUIRED, not optional

In the ts.isCallExpression branch (line 528): delegate to isKnownPureCall. If known pure, check argument purity recursively: return expr.arguments.some(a => expressionHasSideEffects(a, checker)). If not known pure, return true
Thread checker through ALL recursive expressionHasSideEffects calls: binary expressions, prefix/postfix, and the forEachChild fallback

Update ALL call sites to pass checker: extractReturnExpression (thread from translateBody), collectAssignments (already has checker), isGuardStatement (already has checker), variableStatementHasNoSideEffects (add checker param), blockHasNoSideEffects (add checker param), isPureExpression in translate-signature.ts (add checker param if it calls expressionHasSideEffects, or inline the check)
Run npm run test:update-snapshots — expressions-const-pure-calls.ts expectations now show inlined-and-translated output for pure calls; impure call fixture still shows rejected
Verify existing const-binding tests in expressions-const-bindings.ts are unchanged (no calls in those fixtures)
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_GENERAL_CALLS_PATCH_4.

> Purity oracle wired into const-binding inlining.
> Pure calls in const initializers are now inlineable.

ConstBinding.
CallExpression.

pure? c: CallExpression => Bool.
initializer b: ConstBinding => CallExpression.
inlineable? b: ConstBinding => Bool.

---

> Const bindings with pure call initializers are inlineable.
all b: ConstBinding, pure? (initializer b) |
  inlineable? b.

> Const bindings with impure initializers are not inlineable.
all b: ConstBinding, ~pure? (initializer b) |
  ~inlineable? b.

Add them to expressions-calls.ts with their declare'd callees
Run npm run test:update-snapshots
Verify unsupported.ts retains: arrowWithLocals, destructuredParam, multiParamCallback, conditionalAssign, loopAssign

mergabilityInsight: 3 of 5 patches are INFRA. Patches 1 and 3 are independent and can execute in parallel (two tracks). The 2 BEHAVIOR patches are tightly scoped: Patch 2 modifies one function (translateCallExpr), Patch 4 adds one parameter to expressionHasSideEffects and threads it. Total new code: ~1 new file (purity.ts ~150 LOC), ~60 lines changed in translate-body.ts, 2 new fixture files.
finalStateSpec:
module TS2PANT_GENERAL_CALLS.

> After milestone 2: general call translation + purity oracle.
> Ref: Kroening & Strichman, Decision Procedures, Ch. 4 (EUF).
> Ref: Cousot & Cousot, Abstract Interpretation, POPL 1977.
> Ref: Lucassen & Gifford, Polymorphic Effect Systems, POPL 1988.

CallExpression.
ConstBinding.
PurityTier.

builtin-allowlist => PurityTier.
effect-ts-aware => PurityTier.
conservative-default => PurityTier.

special-cased? c: CallExpression => Bool.
has-spread? c: CallExpression => Bool.
identifier-callee? c: CallExpression => Bool.
prop-access-callee? c: CallExpression => Bool.
translated? c: CallExpression => Bool.
rejected? c: CallExpression => Bool.
tier c: CallExpression => PurityTier.
pure? c: CallExpression => Bool.
initializer b: ConstBinding => CallExpression.
inlineable? b: ConstBinding => Bool.

---

> Every call expression has a definite translation outcome.
all c: CallExpression |
  translated? c or rejected? c.

> Special cases always translate.
all c: CallExpression, special-cased? c |
  translated? c.

> General calls with known callee shape and no spread translate.
all c: CallExpression, ~special-cased? c, ~has-spread? c |
  (identifier-callee? c or prop-access-callee? c)
  -> translated? c.

> Spread in non-special calls is rejected.
all c: CallExpression, has-spread? c, ~special-cased? c |
  rejected? c.

> Purity tiers: allowlist and effect-ts are pure.
all c: CallExpression, tier c = builtin-allowlist |
  pure? c.

all c: CallExpression, tier c = effect-ts-aware |
  pure? c.

> Conservative default is not pure.
all c: CallExpression, tier c = conservative-default |
  ~pure? c.

> Const bindings with pure call initializers are inlineable.
all b: ConstBinding, pure? (initializer b) |
  inlineable? b.

> Const bindings with impure initializers are not inlineable.
all b: ConstBinding, ~pure? (initializer b) |
  ~inlineable? b.
